posts.text: We are currently organizing the 12th edition of #dotastro, in New York in early October. Check out the webpage to learn more, and consider signing up! https://www.dotastronomy.com/twelve
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: My passion project alongside my regular work is .Astronomy (#dotastro), a conference series I've co-organised for 10+ years, on innovative uses of the internet, software and technology for astronomy, and how we can use them to blur the lines between research, scicomm & education.
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: Sounds like an awesome #dotastro hack idea 😃
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: The challenge is how you organize that effectively at a 1000+ participant conference? We have done morning runs at dotastro in the past but this is a different scale.
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text:
4. Spent a fantastic week in the mountains in Idaho, and lots of time in nature around Maryland, West Virginia, Virginia and North Carolina 🏔️🌳⛷️. 
5. We had our first in-person dotAstro event since 2019, in New York City and it was SO much fun ✨
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: Great. Will you post about dotAstro or should I repost?
posts.author: @chrislintott.bsky.social

posts.text: Astronomers on Bluesky: come join us at the 13th dotAstro conference at ESAC in Madrid. dotAstro is a inclusive gathering of astronomers from all backgrounds, and at all career stages, who are passionate about how technology and the web can transform our science. Do come if you can.  🛰️🔭
posts.author: @chrislintott.bsky.social

posts.text: The next #dotastro is happening really soon! April, in Madrid 🇪🇸 Register interest and learn more at www.dotastronomy.com/thirteen
posts.author: @orbitingfrog.bsky.social

posts.text: the full program is still being put together but we're going to have some great speakers on data science, AI, open science, tons of unconferences, a hack day, and the opportunity to learn about ESA, ESASky and much much more! #dotastro
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: There's still time to sign up for our next #dotastro event at ESAC in Madrid (23-26 April) - we're close to the deadline and have a great list of participants already, but we have space for a few more people. Send us your interest form asap! docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1F...
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: It’s very new. @vrooje.bsky.social @davidoryan.bsky.social and @emily.space made it at dotastro just a couple of weeks ago.
posts.author: @karenlmasters.bsky.social

posts.text: It certainly helps that the key organiser is a dotastro alumnus :-)
posts.author: @mpoessel.de

posts.text: Great talk at #iauga2024 by #dotastro alum Kartheik Iyer (Columbia) on the J-HIVE project. It brings together ~1 million galaxies from public #JWST data with a uniform reduction and provides a web-based visualization tool. Amazing. j-hive.org 🔭
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

highlights: Sarah Kendrew: "My passion project alongside my regular work is .Astronomy (#dotastro), a conference series I've co-organised for 10+ years"
Markus Poessel connecting dotastro alumni to new events
notes: Posts mentioning dotastro but not tied to a specific event. Includes announcements, reflections, and cross-references from the community.
